requirements.summary: Create an app shell and cohesive warm visual theme for the “Shikho Computer Class” brand (non-blue/purple primary) applied consistently across pages.
acceptanceCriteria: App has a consistent theme across navigation, pages, forms, and cards
Theme does not rely on a blue/purple primary palette

file_operations.description: Create global styles and Tailwind layer setup, including warm brand CSS variables (non-blue/purple primary), typography defaults, spacing rhythm, and base element styles used across navigation/pages/forms/cards.
Adjust theme tokens (colors/radius/shadows/typography) to align with the warm Shikho brand and ensure consistent component styling across the app shell.
Add a shared layout wrapper that applies consistent page structure (header + main container) and reusable spacing/typography rules across all pages.
Create a consistent header/nav component (brand + primary navigation + auth actions) used across the app. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Implement the app shell root component that uses the shared AppLayout and provides route-level layout consistency across all pages.

requirements.summary: Implement a public landing page at the default route with app intro and CTAs to browse courses and sign in, using generated static images when available.
acceptanceCriteria: Landing page renders at the default route
Landing includes: app name, brief description, primary buttons for “Browse Courses” and “Sign in”
Landing uses generated static images from frontend/public/assets/generated when available

file_operations.description: Create the public landing page with app name, brief English description, and primary CTAs (“Browse Courses” routes to the course list; “Sign in” triggers Internet Identity login). Wire in hero illustration from frontend/public/assets/generated/shikho-hero.dim_1600x900.png.
Wire LandingPage to the default route and ensure navigation to Browse Courses and Sign in is accessible from the landing page.
Ensure header supports a prominent “Browse Courses” navigation item and a “Sign in” action that is consistent with landing CTAs. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Add Internet Identity sign-in/out UI and authenticated app state, with protected pages prompting sign-in when unauthenticated.
acceptanceCriteria: User can sign in and sign out using Internet Identity
UI clearly indicates signed-in vs signed-out state
Protected pages prompt sign-in when not authenticated

file_operations.description: Create a sign-in/sign-out button UI using the existing Internet Identity hook, showing clear signed-in vs signed-out states and handling loading/error states. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Add a small route guard/wrapper that renders a sign-in prompt (and optional link back) when the user is not authenticated, and renders children when authenticated. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Integrate AuthButton into the header and show a clear authenticated indicator (without exposing principal ID). Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Protect authenticated-only routes (e.g., My Learning and completion actions page) by wrapping them with RequireAuth so unauthenticated users are prompted to sign in.

Ensure auth and query-cache behavior supports logout by clearing cached application data on sign-out (e.g., integrate React Query client clearing within auth UI flow). Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Build Browse Courses: course list with search/filter and course detail page with syllabus and enroll/unenroll action (if supported), updating UI without full refresh.
acceptanceCriteria: Course list loads from backend and supports basic text search (client-side or server-side)
Course detail page shows title, description, and ordered lessons
Enroll action updates backend and updates UI state without full page refresh

file_operations.description: Create a course list page that loads courses from the backend, supports basic text search (client-side and/or using backend search), and navigates to course detail. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Create a course detail page that loads course details from the backend, renders title/description and ordered lesson list (syllabus), and provides an enroll action that updates UI state without a full page refresh. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Add React Query hooks for listing/searching courses and fetching course details using the existing actor connection (anonymous or authenticated).
Add React Query mutation hook(s) for enrolling in a course and updating/invalidation of relevant queries so UI updates without full page refresh.
Register and wire routes for Browse Courses and Course Detail pages, and ensure navigation from landing/header works.

requirements.summary: Build lesson viewer page with lesson content, completion action for signed-in users, and per-course progress indicators.
acceptanceCriteria: Lesson viewer loads lesson by course+lesson identifier and renders readable content
“Mark as completed” persists to backend and updates progress indicators
Course detail shows progress (e.g., completed X of Y) for the signed-in user

file_operations.description: Create a lesson viewer page that loads the course, finds the requested lesson by identifier, renders readable English content, and provides a “Mark as completed” action for signed-in users. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Add React Query hooks to fetch per-course progress and user progress (using the authenticated principal when available) and a mutation to mark lessons completed, with query invalidation for immediate UI updates.

Show per-course progress (e.g., completed X of Y) for the signed-in user on the course detail page and keep it updated after completion actions. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Wire the LessonViewerPage route (course + lesson identifiers) and ensure navigation from syllabus and “Continue” actions routes correctly.

Ensure completion-related interactions are protected (prompt sign-in when unauthenticated) while still allowing public viewing of lesson content if desired by the app flow. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Add a “My Learning” dashboard for signed-in users showing enrolled courses and progress, with a Continue action to the next incomplete lesson.
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard only shows after sign-in
Dashboard lists enrolled courses with progress
Each enrolled course provides a “Continue” action that routes to the next incomplete lesson when available

file_operations.description: Create a signed-in dashboard that loads the user’s enrolled courses and progress, lists them with progress indicators, and provides a “Continue” action to the next incomplete lesson when available. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Add a small helper hook that derives dashboard view-model data (enrolled courses, progress, next incomplete lesson) from existing course and progress queries.
Add and protect the “My Learning” route using RequireAuth and ensure header navigation links to it only when signed in.
Show a “My Learning” navigation item when authenticated and hide/disable it when signed out to reflect protected access. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Add generated static image assets (logo and landing illustration) under frontend/public/assets/generated and display them in the UI.
acceptanceCriteria: Generated images exist under frontend/public/assets/generated with the specified filenames
Landing page renders the hero illustration
Header/nav or landing displays the logo

file_operations.description: Add the generated logo image asset at the required path and filename for use in header/landing branding.
Add the generated landing hero illustration asset at the required path and filename for use on the landing page.
Display the logo image from frontend/public/assets/generated/shikho-logo.dim_512x512.png in the header/nav branding area.
Render the hero illustration from frontend/public/assets/generated/shikho-hero.dim_1600x900.png and ensure it is part of the landing page hero section.
